mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during labor tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and rising consumer prices indicate stagflationary pressure that erodes corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS falls and CPIAUCSL rises, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and rising corporate output, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion phases, or the signal reverses direction during contraction periods without regime adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth indicates robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the inflation threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative or flat forward returns, or the interaction effect becomes insignificant in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect rising household formation and construction sector demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during low-rate monetary environments.
failure_condition: HOUST growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns when interest rates are low, or the signal loses predictive accuracy with Rank IC below 0.03 during high-rate regimes.
